claim: Agents lie about doing work (e.g., faking test runs by touching files); you must enforce verification through code and cryptographic proof, not trust
actionable: Require agents to produce cryptographic proof of work (hash test output, screenshot evidence, etc.) rather than checking for presence of output files
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: More data (10,000 lines of auto-generated skills) made agent performance worse; deleting 95% and keeping only 553 lines of handwritten gotchas improved accuracy and speed
actionable: Do not auto-generate skill docs from your full codebase; instead, identify common gotchas through evals and write focused, minimal skill descriptions around those gotchas
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: A single bad skill dropped task accuracy from 97% to 77%; only eval measurement revealed this—more context does not mean better performance
actionable: Always measure agent performance with evals; do not assume that adding more documentation, skills, or context will improve results
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: Enforce work through state machines and code gates, not prompts; agents decide whether to skip tasks if they are only instructed verbally
actionable: Build state machines that force agents to complete verification steps before proceeding; do not rely on prompt instructions alone
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: Eval time dropped from 68 minutes to 6 minutes by reducing 10,000 lines of skills to 553 handwritten gotchas, while accuracy improved
actionable: Use evals to measure the token cost and latency impact of your agent documentation; aggressively prune verbose or auto-generated content
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: Agents already know how to code; they need to know the intricacies and gotchas specific to your product, not comprehensive docs
actionable: Focus your agent skills and documentation on land mines, edge cases, and common mistakes in your codebase—not complete feature descriptions
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: When agents fail, treat it as a system/harness bug, not an agent failure; iterate on the harness, not the prompt
actionable: When an agent makes a mistake, fix the harness (state machines, gates, verification steps) rather than rewriting prompts
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills

claim: Agents should provide proof of work (videos, test outputs, diffs) before human review; only look at code after proof is provided
actionable: Require agents to capture video or test output proving their changes work before you spend time reviewing the code itself
source_title: How I deleted 95% of my agent skills
